description: The provided image pixel displays a publisher status badge with no extractable scientific chart or micrograph. Relying on the accompanying paper context regarding Fe–28Mn–9Al–1.2C-(0, 3, 6, 9)Cr austenitic low-density steel, the investigated figure likely presents data from near-rapid solidified strips in as-cast and annealed (550°C, 5 h) states. The alloy system consists of an austenite matrix with δ-ferrite increasing from 10.2 vol% to 19.6 vol% as Cr rises, accompanied by suppressed nano-scale κ-carbides, DO3 ordering in ferrite, and Cr23C6 boundary precipitation in 9Cr. Mechanical trends show as-cast yield strengths of 724, 617, 652, and 740 MPa for 0Cr through 9Cr, with annealed 6Cr achieving the highest elongation of 44.3 ± 3%. Strain hardening rates progressively increase with Cr content, while annealing reduces dislocation density and refines slip band spacing, underscoring the trade-off between carbide suppression and ferrite stabilization.
